ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jul-11-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amanda Ebey, via Zoom, for Carmen McMahan, Personal Representative; Patricia Hewlett, in court, in propria persona, Objector.

The court summarizes the prior calling of this matter and advises Ms. Ebey that it has reviewed her objection to Ms. Hewlett's peremptory challenge and reminds the parties that Judge Quinn has already recused himself. Ms. Ebey submits. Judge Moody advises the parties that for the record, he is granting the peremptory challenge as of today, Jul-11-2022 and accordingly this matter will be referred to the Dept. of the Presiding Judge (Dept. 206) for assignment to a different Judge. The parties should expect notice regarding the preceding from Dept. 206.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Saephan. Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-13-2022 Continued To Jul-11-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Patricia Hewlett, in court, in propria persona, Objector; Amanda Ebey, via Zoom, for Carmen McMahan, Personal Representative.

The Judge acknowledges the peremptory challenge filed by Ms. Hewlett and advises that he intends to accept the challenge and recuse himself. Ms. Ebey advises that this matter has been delayed several times and that Ms. Hewlett has no standing in this matter as she is neither an heir at law nor a beneficiary. Ms. Ebey objects to the peremptory challenge and points the court to her objection thereon that she filed on 6/10/2022.

After additional discussion, the Judge advises Ms. Hewlett that he is not accepting her peremptory challenge at this time. The matter will be continued to Jul-11-2022 at 9:00 am. The court instructs Ms. Hewlett to file formal objections on or before Jun-27-2022.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-29-2021 Continued To May-17-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amanda Ebey appeared by zoom for Carmen McMahan (Administrator).

The Court stated that it does not have a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration. Ms. Ebey stated that she would like to avoid doing a status report and file a final petition, that she is waiting for some information from her Client as to whether they need a reserve, that the sole heir passed away and requested 30 to 60-days to file a final petition. Status on close of estate is continued to May 17, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Vedica Puri. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (3653-3655 16th Street, San Francisco, Ca)
Off Calendar Nov-16-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amanda Ebey for Carmen McMahan (Administrator).
Francisco Gutierrez appeared by zoom for Ana Campos-Sazo.

The estate owns a 50% interest in the property. The sale is for 100% of the property. The sale was confirmed at $927,912.50 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed September 28, 2020. The Court grants the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property. An additional bond is not required. The matter is under submission for the purpose of reviewing the order.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Helen Trowbridge. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition Of Personal Representative For Order For Court To Retain Jurisdiction Pending Appeal For Authoirzation Ot Manage And Sell Real Property And For Order Requiring Vexatious Litigant To Obtain Prefiling Order
Off Calendar Dec-12-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Evyn Shomer for San Francisco Public Guardian ("PG").
Francisco Gutierrez for Maria Campos.
Amanda Ebey for Carmen McMahan.
Patricia Hewlett in Propria Persona.

The Court questions why they are seeking power to open an account and manage the property when it is already encompassed in limited authority. Ms. Ebey pointed out that there is a stay of appointment of Ms. McMahan. Ms. Shomer stated that the Public Guardian is the temporary Conservator of Henry, that they have continued to receive rents pending authority for Carmen to receive the payments and that they are willing to give the rents to her. Ms. Hewlett stated that she is the property manager, that her authority was taken from her illegally and that she is hiring an Attorney. Ms. Hewlett also spoke of the Ex-Wives, family dynamics, the Court of Appeal ruling, a revocable living trust and stated that she will be contacting the Attorney where Mr. Solorzano had papers. The Court stated that it will not issue an order to the Court of Appeal. The Petition is granted in part (subject to review and modification of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jun-12-2017 Denied

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jun-19-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ross McLauran Madden for and with petitioner Patricia Hewlett; Amanda Ebey for petitioner Carmen McMahan; Evyn Shomer for San Francisco Public Guarduan as conservator of Henry Solarzano.

Mr. Madden stated that Ms. Hewlettt filed her petition with the knowledge and consent of Henry Solarzano prior to the order appointing a conservator for Mr. Solarzano. He stated that Mr. Solarzano's subsequent petition to have himself appointed administrator of the estate occurred after the conservatorship, and that Mr. Solarzano was not competent to serve in that capacity. Ms. Ebey agreed that Mr. Solarzino was not competent to serve as the administrator of his son's estate, and contended that Ms. Hewlett was not suitable or appropriate to serve in that capacity as well, as she is under investigation for elder abuse as to Henry Solarzano. She added that petitioner Carmen McMahan is the sister of the decedent and has statutory priority for appointment as administrator of her brother's estate in light of their father's incompetency. Ms. Shomer stated that the Public Guardian had been appointed as conservator of Henry Solarzano on June 1, 2017 in related action PCN-17-300709, and that it was the position of the Public Guardian that Ms. Hewlett was not appropriate for appoinment as estate representative herein. She stated that there has been a co-minglung of funds by Ms. Hewlett in violation of her fiduciary duties under her appointment as attorney in fact, and that she has caused Mr. Sierra, a person unrelated to Mr. Solarzano, to move into his home. She stated that the order appointing the Public Guardian as conservator specifically provided a restriction over contact from Ms. Hewlett and Mr. Sierra to Mr. Solarzano. She added that having appeared as attorney for Henry Solarzano, Mr. Madden has a conflict of interest now in his representation of Patricia Hewlett as well. Mr. Madden responded that Ms. Hewlett has been the partner of Henty Solarzano for the last five years, and that her appointment is in accordance with the wishes of Mr. Solarzano as expressed by him while he was still competent.
The matters were submitted for desision. The Court ruled that in the interest of protecting the estate, that he was granting the petition of Carmen McMahan to be appointed as administrator of the estate of Richard Solarzano, subject to review of the order. The Petition of Patricia Hewlett was denied.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Court Examiner: Sandra Hilton. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-06-2017 Continued To Apr-10-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:Ross Madden for petitioner Patricia Hewlett and also with Henry Solorzano; Amanda Ebey for and with objector Carmen McMahon, who appeared by CourtCall.

In response to the Court's inquiry, Mr. Madden confirmed that the filing fee for the opposition has been paid. Ms. Ebey also confirmed that fees for the opposition were paid. Mr. Madden stated that Ms. Hewlett intended to dismiss her petition, and that a new petition would be filed by Henry Solorzano. Ms. Ebey stated that a continuance was needed to give appropriate notice. She added that she would be bringing a petition for conservatorship over Henry Solorzano. The matter was continued to April 10, 2017 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-25-2017 Continued To Mar-06-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ross Madden for and with Patricia Hewlett.
Carmen McMahan (Half Sister of Decedent/Daughter of Henry Solorzano) appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

Ms. McMahan stated that she is concerned about her Father, that there is an open Adult Protective Services investigation (Investigator John Foecke), that Richard died twenty years ago, and spoke of her Father's medical condition and his inability to understand the petition. Ms. McMahan also requested a 30-day continuance stating that she is in the process of interviewing an Attorney. Mr. Madden spoke of the conversation he had with Henry, stated that a one quarter interest is deeded to the new deceased ex-wife and that he filed a supplemental declaration addressing the issues raised in the Examiner's notes (copy provided in open Court). The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to March 3, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: L. Maloney. Not reported.
